data.body:
It's my firm opinion that people fabricate narratives to justify their place in the world. Reality has no inherent narrative, yet we're storytelling creatures, so we tend to invent comforting stories where none exist.

Saying "progress is linear" helps us feel better about when we were born.

data.body:
The meaning and origin of the magic word "abracadabra" is hotly debated. Its first known mention was in a 2nd-century medical text by the Roman physician Serenus Sammonicus, who said malaria sufferers should wear an amulet with "abracadabra" written in the form of a triangle.

data.body:
Put 400g cubed potato & 4 chicken thighs (bone-in, skin-on) in roasting tin
Add salt, pepper, 1 tsp fennel seeds, & a little olive oil to coat potatoes
Roast, Fan 180, 30 min
Add 1/2 lemon in wedges, Fan 180, 15 min
Add asparagus, dot 40g wild garlic butter over, Fan 200, 10 min
Serve with lemon
